item.title: The Underworld Market to Remove the Recording Indicator Light on Meta Glasses 摘要
item.description:
A growing underground market on Facebook Marketplace offers to disable the recording indicator light on Ray-Ban Meta glasses, a modification called 'Stealth Mode.' Journalist Joanna Stern paid $100 for the service and investigated the business of turning smart glasses into covert cameras. The irony is that Meta's own platform is used to find hackers who modify Meta's devices for surreptitious recording. The article explores who performs these modifications, their legality, and efforts to stop them.
标签: smart glasses, privacy, hacking, Meta, Facebook Marketplace, covert recording, modification, surveillance, ethics, technology

item.title: CBS News Fires Scott Pelley of ‘60 Minutes’ 摘要
item.description:
CBS News fired veteran '60 Minutes' correspondent Scott Pelley, citing termination for cause. Pelley had criticized new management for incompetence and bias, alleging pressure to insert bias into stories. He expressed deep devotion to the broadcast despite risking his life in war zones. The firing followed a staff meeting where Pelley's comments were disputed by Nick Bilton, whose letter confirming the termination was obtained by The New York Times. Pelley plans to provide details about alleged bias in specific segments.
标签: CBS News, Scott Pelley, 60 Minutes, journalism, media, firing, bias, Nick Bilton, New York Times, news

item.title: My GNU Emacs completion setup (as of June 2026) 摘要
item.description:
The author describes their current Emacs completion setup, which uses multiple packages for minibuffer and in-buffer completion. For minibuffer, they use vertico (candidate display), marginalia (annotations), orderless (filtering), and consult (context). For in-buffer completion, they use completion-preview-mode (inline preview) and corfu (popup completion). They note that this modular approach is typical of modern Emacs, where narrowly focused packages handle specific aspects of a larger feature.
标签: Emacs, completion, vertico, marginalia, orderless, consult, corfu, completion-preview-mode, minibuffer, in-buffer

item.title: Now that your newsletter is AI-generated, I've Unsubscribed 摘要
item.description:
The author reflects on why they unsubscribe from newsletters that switch to AI-generated content without notice, emphasizing the value of human voice and authentic experience. They contrast this with a touching story of a newsletter continued by the author's son after his death, which they stayed subscribed to until the end. The key difference is between content that feels like genuine human thought and content that feels like a monetization strategy.
标签: newsletters, AI content, authenticity, human voice, trust, writing, monetization, personal connection, unsubscribe, storytelling

item.title: The ‘60 Minutes’ Purge 摘要
item.description:
Paramount's Press Express page for 60 Minutes still lists all eight correspondents from the 2025–2026 season, but in reality, four have left: Scott Pelley was fired, Anderson Cooper left after 20 years, and Sharyn Alfonsi and Cecilia Vega were fired last week. The show's brand of stability is eroding under new CBS owner David Ellison and editor-in-chief Bari Weiss, who have fired or lost half the on-air talent and replaced longtime executive producer Tanya Simon with Nick Bilton, a TV news novice. Correspondents Lesley Stahl (84) and Bill Whitaker (74) may also leave, as Scott Pelley described the changes as 'the murder of the institution.'
标签: 60 Minutes, CBS News, media layoffs, David Ellison, Bari Weiss, Scott Pelley, Nick Bilton, journalism, corporate interference, news industry

item.title: Scott Pelley on Leaving ‘60 Minutes’: ‘Incompetence and Unprofessionalism in the New > Management Have Wreaked Havoc’ 摘要
item.description:
Scott Pelley announced his departure from CBS after 37 years, citing the new management's unethical directives, including injecting falsehoods into stories and allowing politicians to choose correspondents. He described the firing of senior leadership and correspondents as cruel and politically motivated, aimed at currying favor with the Trump administration. Pelley lamented the loss of integrity, quality, and humanity at 60 Minutes, stating that the principles he held dear are gone. He expressed gratitude for his colleagues and hoped for a return to sanity, competence, and courage in journalism.
标签: journalism, media, CBS, 60 Minutes, ethics, politics, Trump administration, integrity, news, resignation

item.title: Is datacentre sovereignty really that important? 摘要
item.description:
The author argues that the UK's push to build large AI datacentres domestically is misguided. Latency is not a significant issue since AI response times dwarf network delays, and European datacentres suffice. Tax revenue from datacentres is minimal (0.2% of government spending) and jobs are few. The argument for control is weak: private operators serve global demand, models rapidly depreciate, and seizure is impractical due to remote wiping and supply chain dependencies. Instead, the UK should leverage its existing strengths in AI research, talent, and funding rather than focusing on physical infrastructure.
标签: AI, datacentres, UK, policy, latency, tax, sovereignty, cloud, infrastructure, economics
